---
pagetitle: "Hugging Face - Architecture 2.0"
description: "Placeholder artifact page for the Architecture 2.0 Hugging Face collection."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="site-wrap site-narrow">
<header class="site-masthead">
  <span class="pill">Architecture 2.0</span>
  <span class="pill hot">Artifacts</span>
</header>

<section class="site-hero">
  <div class="hub-kicker">Hugging Face</div>
  <h1>Artifact hub</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">A credit-preserving home for datasets, models, examples, and reusable loop artifacts</p>
</section>

<section class="community-panel" id="collection">
  <div>
    <span class="site-note-label">Collection pending</span>
    <h2>🤗 Hugging Face collection coming soon</h2>
    <p>
      The Architecture 2.0 Hugging Face space will collect artifacts that help
      people reproduce and extend AI-assisted architecture loops. It is meant to
      point to community work, preserve author and institution credit, and make
      reusable assets easier to discover.
    </p>
  </div>
  <nav class="site-actions" aria-label="Artifact actions">
    <a class="site-action disabled" href="#collection" aria-disabled="true">Collection coming soon</a>
    <a class="site-action secondary" href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/architecture20/discussions">Discuss artifact plans</a>
  </nav>
</section>

<section class="site-section">
  <div class="section-heading">
    <h2>What belongs here</h2>
    <p>The collection should make artifacts visible without taking credit away from the people who created them.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="site-strands cols-2">
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Data</span>
      <h3>Datasets and traces</h3>
      <p>Workload traces, benchmark packets, simulator outputs, and evidence datasets with clear provenance.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Models</span>
      <h3>Models and proxies</h3>
      <p>Surrogate models, learned cost models, predictors, and checkpoints that support reviewable architecture loops.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Examples</span>
      <h3>Runnable examples</h3>
      <p>Small loop demos, notebooks, cards, and evidence ledgers that help newcomers understand how the pieces fit.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="strand">
      <span class="site-pill">Credit</span>
      <h3>Attribution first</h3>
      <p>Every listed artifact should preserve authors, institutions, source links, licenses, papers, and preferred citations.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="provenance">
  Until the collection opens, propose artifacts through
  <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/architecture20/discussions">GitHub Discussions</a>
  or submit related tools through the
  <a href="https://github.com/harvard-edge/architecture20/issues/new?template=submit_tool.yml">tool template</a>.
</section>
</div>
```
